rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: J.B.R. ENTERPREISES, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The City And County Of San Francisco Department Of Aging And Adult Services
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONERS J.B.R. ENTERPREISES, LLC, JAY PENN'S Motion To Compel The City And County Of San Francisco Department Of Aging And Adult Services To Comply With Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear, hearing required. Prior to the hearing, the parties are required to meet and confer (in person, if feasible) about the terms and scope of a potential protective order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LOANME, INC. A NEVADA CORPORATION FKA VS. LOCAL TASTES OF THE CITY TOURS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Award Of Post-Judgment Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF LOANME, INC. A NEVADA CORPORATION FKA's Motion For Award Of Post-Judgment Attorneys Fees Plaintiff LoanMe, Inc.'s motion for award of post-judgment attorneys' fees is granted in part. Paragraph 9 of the Forbearance Agreement plainly states that Plaintiff is entitled to attorneys' fees because of Defendants Local Tastes of the City Tours, LLC and Thomas Medin's default. However, CCP section 685.040 only entitles a judgment creditor to reasonable and necessary costs of enforcing a judgment. Despite the broad language of Paragraph 9 indicating a right to retroactive fees, the fees incurred prior to May 9, 2016 were not reasonable and necessary costs of enforcing the judgment because the default judgment had not yet been entered. Deducting the actual costs incurred before this date, rather than the initial attorneys' fees award, results in a new award of $4905.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT GABRIELA CARDONA'S Motion For Order To Compel Responses For Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Bank Of America, N.A, And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. Though the plaintiff indicates in its papers that substantially compliant responses will be served, the Defendant's reply indicates that no such services has taken place. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,810.00, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answers
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTs GERALD NIESAR, OSCAR ESCOBAR, and NIESAR & VESTAL, LLP's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answers Defendants Niesar & Vestal, LLP, Gerald Niesar, and Oscar Escobar's motion for leave to file first amended answers is GRANTED. Courts exercise liberality in permitting amendments at any stage of the proceeding and particularly use a liberal policy in allowing amendments to answers. (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 ("In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense.").) While Defendants appear to have overlooked the fact that the waiver was in their possession prior to Plaintiffs' production of documents, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated that they will be prejudiced by allowing an amendment to the answer at this stage of litigation, while discovery is ongoing and trial is still five months away. Waxman v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County (1966) 246 Cal.App.2d 668 is distinguishable. In Waxman, the defendant sought to amend his answer 17 months after filing the original answer. (Id. at 671.) The defendant failed to explain why the affirmative defense was not included in the original answer, discovery had been completed, and the new defense would have required additional discovery that would have delayed trial. (Id.) By contrast, Defendants here brought this motion under a year after filing the original answer and they included an explanation that they overlooked the waiver and did not understand its import until receiving it in the context of Plaintiffs' responses. Plaintiffs have not indicated what new discovery would be required to respond to the waiver defense or how this amendment would prejudice them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: HIRECLOUT, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. HONOR TECHNOLOGY INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Honor Technology, Inc. For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT HONOR TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion Of Honor Technology, Inc. For Summary Judgment Defendant Honor Technology, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff performed the contract by properly representing Ms. Long to Honor as a candidate with Android experience. (James Dec., pars. 2-6.) If the trier of fact concludes that plaintiff performed, then it is entitled to its fee because Honor hired Ms. Long within a year of the reference. rulings.Case Title: CARL A WESCOTT VS. KATHY LEE TROSCLAIR ESQ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT's Mtn To Amend Complaint Plaintiff's motion to amend complaint is denied. The motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324. From what the court can discern, it appears that plaintiff seeks to add a cause of action for violation of Business and Professions code sec. 6455. That section allows a claim where there is a disclosure of confidential information. The proposed allegations at most allege a threat to disclose-which is not actionable-rather than an actual disclosure. The proposed complaint also fails to allege facts showing "outrageous conduct" to support the proposed intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Defendants' request for fees per CCP 128.5 is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: MERRILEE HARRIS VS. COLINDRES & ASSOCIATES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Merrilee Harris'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Cross-Complainant Colindres And Associates, Inc. And Its Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF MERRILEE HARRIS' Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Cross-Complainant Colindres And Associates, Inc. And Its Attorneys Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar. Motion withdrawn on October 23, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA KOSSIAVELOS VS. STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas; Request For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2546.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Eduardo A. Brito; And Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MARIA KOSSIAVELOS'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas; Request For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2546.00 Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED as moot. The Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Normally, some amount of sanctions would be appropriate. The circumstances of this case call for a different result. In their reply brief, the Plaintiff asserts that the press of time required the short window for response to Plaintiff counsel's original meet and confer letters prior to filing this motion. In particular, counsel noted the soon-approaching return date on the subject subpoenas. However, the subpoenas appear to have been originally signed on September 10, a full 17 days before counsel sent his first meet and confer letters. Accordingly, it appears that counsel bears at least some responsibility for the professed press of time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY FISCHER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant CCSF's demurrer to the second and third causes of action are sustained without leave to amend because they fail to state a cause of action. (CCP § 430.10(e).) Plaintiff complains of a dangerous condition of public property and his exclusive remedy is to pursue a claim under Gov't Code § 835. (Amended Complaint, par. 6; Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 829; see also Longfellow v. County of San Luis Obispo (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 379, 383-385.) In addition, plaintiff cannot pursue causes of action two and three per Gov't Code § 815.6. The purpose of the predicate statutes (Street and Highways Code §§ 5611 and 5615 and SF Department of Public Works Code § 706.3) is to allow CCSF to recoup its costs when it makes repairs. Any benefit to plaintiff is incidental. (See Haggis v. City of Los Angeles (2000) 22 Cal.4th 490; 499; Schaefer v. Lenahan (1944) 63 Cal.App.2d 324, 327, 332.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: JAMES POZZI VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE 's Motion For Change Of Venue Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for change of venue is DENIED. There is a presumption in favor of the plaintiff's choice of forum. (Bechtel Corp. v. Superior Court (1973) 33 Cal.App.3d 405, 407, n.1.) Defendant does not contest that venue is proper is San Francisco County and it has failed to meet its burden under CCP section 397(c) that changing the venue would be in the interests of justice or for the convenience of the witnesses. (Smith v. Stanford Research Institute (1963) 212 Cal.App.2d 750, 754 ("[A] corporate defendant seeking a change of venue has the burden of negating the propriety of venue laid on all possible grounds.").) A party moving for change of venue is required to use supporting declarations setting forth witnesses, the nature of the testimony, and the reasons why they would be inconvenienced by the current forum. (Corfee v. Southern California Edison (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) Defendant has produced no declarations regarding what witnesses it plans to use and how those witnesses would be inconvenienced by the case continuing in San Francisco. By contrast, Plaintiff has produced a declaration from its chief witness, Richard Reynolds, explaining the importance of his testimony in the action and why he would be inconvenienced by a change of venue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: STEFAN STEFANOV VS. THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 6, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANTs THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY LLC and WESTERN ATHLETIC CLUBS LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration Defendants The Bay Clubs Company LLC and Western Athletic Clubs LLC's motion to compel arbitration is DENIED. Defendants have failed to show the existence of a valid agreement to arbitrate plaintiff Stefan Stefanov's claims. The language of the 2016 arbitration agreement is materially different from the language of the 2015 employment agreement with respect to the employee's right to opt out of arbitration. The language of the 2015 employment agreement states that Stefanov may "opt out of arbitration" if he does not wish to participate without any qualification of that right. The 2016 arbitration agreement states that it is a condition of employment and provides that the employee has the right to opt only of the PAGA claims. The arbitration agreement does not purport to rescind the 2015 employment agreement and attempts to modify the terms of employment without satisfying the conditions prescribed in the employment agreement for their effective change. The parties agreed on the method to amend the conditions of employment and the purported 2016 arbitration agreement is not a valid amendment. (See Rebolledo v. Tilly's, Inc. (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 900, 924.) Therefore, the 2016 agreement does not change Mr. Stefanov's right to opt out of arbitration of his disputes with his employer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb
